steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to explain WHY brine pools are toxic to most sea life. A good answer must give the cause (extreme saltiness) and connect it to toxicity.

steps.title: Match the notes to the goal
steps.content: The notes say the water is three to eight times saltier than the surrounding ocean and that this extreme saltiness is what makes it toxic. Choice B states both halves of that cause-and-effect in one sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'some bacteria can survive there': notes an exception to the toxicity but never explains the cause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'toxic to most sea life because it is three to eight times saltier': gives the salinity cause and the toxicity effect. ✓
  • Choice C — 'discovered by researchers in 2022': states the toxicity but explains the discovery, not the reason. ✗
  • Choice D — 'salty underwater lakes that sit on the ocean floor': defines a brine pool without mentioning toxicity at all. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
